text:
Roy Parsons is the eponymous character of the virtual reality arcade game "Roy: A Life Well Lived" which is played by both Morty and Rick at the intergalactic arcade Blips and Chitz in the episode "Mortynight Run".

Roy's personality depends on whoever is playing the game. Regardless, most people forget about their real life and believe themselves to be Roy as long as Roy remains alive. Some people, such as Rick Sanchez, are able to remain conscious both inside and outside the game at the same time, effectively giving Roy their own personalities. This is most likely due to Rick's status as a megagenius, implying highly intelligent individuals are able to remember their real life as they play.

Roy's biography is not predetermined, as he is a video game character and his story is entirely dependent on the player's decisions.

Roy Parsons (C-131)

However, when Morty played as Roy in the episode "Mortynight Run," Roy first appeared as a child who had a nightmare. His mother reassured him, and he went to school. While there, Roy looked out the window during class and saw other kids playing American football. This inspired him to take up the sport and later join the school team. At one of the matches, he caught the eye of a girl in the stands named Rosy. They fell in love immediately and later married, having an unnamed son. However, a few years later, they ran into financial problems. Rosy told Roy he needed to be more realistic and talk to her father about the carpet store. He later developed cancer, and Rosy supported him throughout his treatment. After his recovery, she brought her wheelchair-bound husband home, where she and other family members celebrated his recovery. One workday, one of his colleagues asked him to move a Persian carpet. Roy climbed the ladder, but suddenly fell and died.

In the "Rick and Morty Presents: Mr. Meeseeks" comic, one of the Mr. Meeseekses decides to play as him. He wakes up and goes to help his mom make chili. A few years later, he began playing American football at the school. He later had a falling out with his father over football. There he met Rosy. They began spending time together and later began dating. She later accused Roy of cheating on her, and they broke up. He later dropped out of school and became effectively unemployed. He began to spend his time in bars, drinking heavily, out of grief. However, Roy found the strength to overcome his alcohol addiction and remained sober for at least a year. He unexpectedly ran into Rosy, and they decided to eat together at a restaurant. She was surprised he didn't order chili; he said people change. Rosy told him she thought love was her destiny. However, she later realized that love grows when two caring people share connection and understanding. Suddenly, a ventilation shaft fell from the ceiling onto her, and she died. Mr. Meeseeks, playing as Roy, was surprised by her death but was glad that death was possible in this game. He killed Roy by stabbing him, after which he played the game many times, dying in different ways.

Roy Parsons (Parmesan Dimension)

In the episode "Rick: A Mort Well Lived", Rick plays as the character in order to save Morty and get him out of the game after he is trapped in the game when his mind becomes splintered into all of the NPC characters in the game.
